#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
novel_reader.py — Fetch a web novel chapter from a URL and read it aloud
in a natural, human-like voice.

Uses:
  - trafilatura / BeautifulSoup  -> extract the actual chapter text (strips
    nav bars, ads, "next chapter" links, comment sections, etc.)
  - edge-tts                    -> free, high-quality neural TTS (no API key)
  - pydub + ffmpeg              -> stitch chunks together with natural pauses

Install once:
    pip install edge-tts trafilatura beautifulsoup4 requests pydub --break-system-packages
    (also need ffmpeg installed on your system: `sudo apt install ffmpeg` /
     `brew install ffmpeg` / choco on Windows)

Usage:
    python novel_reader.py "<chapter_url>"
    python novel_reader.py "<chapter_url>" --voice en-US-AndrewNeural --rate +5% --play
    python novel_reader.py "<chapter_url>" --output chapter1.mp3

List available voices:
    python novel_reader.py --list-voices
"""

import argparse
import asyncio
import re
import sys
import tempfile
import os
from pathlib import Path

import requests
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

try:
    import trafilatura
except ImportError:
    trafilatura = None

import edge_tts
from pydub import AudioSegment

HEADERS = {
    "User-Agent": (
        "Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 "
        "(KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/124.0 Safari/537.36"
    )
}

# A few voices that sound notably natural / "human" rather than flat.
RECOMMENDED_VOICES = {
    "male": "en-US-AndrewNeural",
    "female": "en-US-AvaNeural",
    "male_warm": "en-US-BrianNeural",
    "female_warm": "en-US-EmmaNeural",
    "male_uk": "en-GB-RyanNeural",
    "female_uk": "en-GB-SoniaNeural",
}

NOISE_SELECTORS = [
    "script", "style", "nav", "header", "footer", "aside", "form",
    "iframe", "noscript", "button", "svg",
]
# Common junk phrases that leak in even after extraction on novel sites.
NOISE_PATTERNS = [
    r"advertisement", r"report chapter", r"next chapter", r"previous chapter",
    r"read more chapters?.*", r"bookmark this page", r"^\s*chapter list\s*$",
    r"please disable your ad.?blocker", r"if you.*enjoy.*support.*author",
    # Patterns specific to the wuxiaspot.com / fanmtl.com template family
    # (and the many near-identical "MTL aggregator" clones that share it).
    r"^default$", r"^dyslexic$", r"^roboto$", r"^lora$",
    r"^prev$", r"^next$", r"^index$", r"chevron_left", r"chevron_right",
    r"^nights_stay$", r"^home$",
    r"tap the screen to use advanced tools",
    r"you can use left and right keyboard keys",
    r"^search$", r"^categories$", r"^tags$", r"^updates$",
    r"your (fictional|fan-fiction) stories hub",
]

# Once any of these show up, everything from that line onward is trailing
# boilerplate ("You'll Also Like" recommendations, footer links, etc.) —
# cut the text there rather than trying to filter each line individually.
TRAILING_CUTOFFS = [
    r"you'?ll also like",
    r"^\s*###?\s*user comments",
    r"^\s*###?\s*hot keywords",
]


def fetch_html(url: str) -> str:
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=HEADERS, timeout=20)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    return resp.text


# CSS selectors seen on the wuxiaspot.com / fanmtl.com template family and
# common lookalikes. Tried before falling back to generic heuristics.
SITE_TEMPLATE_SELECTORS = [
    "#chapter-content", ".chapter-content", ".chapter-c",
    "#chr-content", ".chr-c", ".txt", "#content-area",
    "article .content", ".reading-content",
]


def extract_with_known_selectors(html: str) -> str | None:
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    for tag in soup(NOISE_SELECTORS):
        tag.decompose()
    for selector in SITE_TEMPLATE_SELECTORS:
        node = soup.select_one(selector)
        if node:
            paragraphs = node.find_all("p")
            if paragraphs:
                text = "\n\n".join(p.get_text(" ", strip=True) for p in paragraphs)
            else:
                text = node.get_text("\n\n", strip=True)
            if len(text) > 200:
                return text
    return None


def extract_with_trafilatura(html: str) -> str | None:
    if trafilatura is None:
        return None
    text = trafilatura.extract(
        html,
        include_comments=False,
        include_tables=False,
        no_fallback=False,
    )
    return text


def extract_with_bs4_heuristic(html: str) -> str:
    """Fallback: find the element with the most paragraph text — usually
    the chapter body on novel-reading sites."""
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    for tag in soup(NOISE_SELECTORS):
        tag.decompose()

    candidates = soup.find_all(["div", "article", "section"])
    best, best_len = None, 0
    for c in candidates:
        text = c.get_text(" ", strip=True)
        if len(text) > best_len:
            best, best_len = c, len(text)

    if best is None:
        return soup.get_text(" ", strip=True)

    paragraphs = best.find_all("p")
    if paragraphs:
        return "\n\n".join(p.get_text(" ", strip=True) for p in paragraphs)
    return best.get_text("\n\n", strip=True)


def clean_text(raw: str) -> str:
    lines = [ln.strip() for ln in raw.splitlines()]
    cleaned = []
    for ln in lines:
        if not ln:
            continue
        low = ln.lower()
        # Stop entirely once we hit trailing boilerplate (recommendation
        # blocks, comment sections, etc.) — nothing useful comes after.
        if any(re.search(pat, low) for pat in TRAILING_CUTOFFS):
            break
        if any(re.search(pat, low) for pat in NOISE_PATTERNS):
            continue
        if len(ln) < 3:
            continue
        cleaned.append(ln)
    text = "\n\n".join(cleaned)
    text = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", text)
    text = re.sub(r"[ \t]{2,}", " ", text)
    return text.strip()


def extract_chapter_title(html: str) -> str | None:
    soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser")
    h2 = soup.find("h2")
    if h2 and h2.get_text(strip=True):
        return h2.get_text(strip=True)
    title_tag = soup.find("title")
    if title_tag and title_tag.get_text(strip=True):
        return title_tag.get_text(strip=True).split("|")[-1].strip()
    return None


def get_chapter_text(url: str) -> tuple[str, str | None]:
    print(f"Fetching: {url}")
    html = fetch_html(url)

    title = extract_chapter_title(html)

    text = extract_with_known_selectors(html)
    if not text:
        text = extract_with_trafilatura(html)
    if not text or len(text) < 200:
        print("  (falling back to heuristic scan)")
        text = extract_with_bs4_heuristic(html)

    text = clean_text(text)
    if len(text) < 100:
        raise RuntimeError(
            "Couldn't find a substantial block of chapter text on this page. "
            "The site's layout may need a custom selector."
        )
    return text, title


def chunk_text(text: str, max_chars: int = 1400) -> list[str]:
    """Split on paragraph boundaries so pauses land naturally, keeping each
    chunk under max_chars (edge-tts is happier with smaller pieces)."""
    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
    chunks, current = [], ""
    for para in paragraphs:
        if len(current) + len(para) + 2 <= max_chars:
            current = f"{current}\n\n{para}" if current else para
        else:
            if current:
                chunks.append(current)
            current = para
    if current:
        chunks.append(current)
    return chunks


async def synthesize_chunk(text: str, voice: str, rate: str, out_path: str):
    communicate = edge_tts.Communicate(text, voice=voice, rate=rate)
    await communicate.save(out_path)


async def synthesize_all(chunks: list[str], voice: str, rate: str, tmp_dir: str) -> list[str]:
    paths = []
    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        out_path = os.path.join(tmp_dir, f"part_{i:04d}.mp3")
        print(f"  Synthesizing part {i + 1}/{len(chunks)}...")
        await synthesize_chunk(chunk, voice, rate, out_path)
        paths.append(out_path)
    return paths


def stitch_audio(paths: list[str], pause_ms: int = 450) -> AudioSegment:
    silence = AudioSegment.silent(duration=pause_ms)
    combined = AudioSegment.empty()
    for i, p in enumerate(paths):
        combined += AudioSegment.from_mp3(p)
        if i != len(paths) - 1:
            combined += silence
    return combined


def list_voices():
    async def _list():
        voices = await edge_tts.list_voices()
        for v in voices:
            if v["Locale"].startswith("en-"):
                print(f"{v['ShortName']:30s} {v['Gender']:8s} {v['Locale']}")
    asyncio.run(_list())


def main():
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Read a web novel chapter aloud.")
    parser.add_argument("url", nargs="?", help="Chapter URL")
    parser.add_argument("--voice", default=RECOMMENDED_VOICES["male_warm"],
                         help="edge-tts voice name (default: %(default)s). "
                              "Use --list-voices to see options.")
    parser.add_argument("--rate", default="+0%",
                         help="Speech rate adjustment, e.g. +10%%, -15%% (default: +0%%)")
    parser.add_argument("--output", default=None,
                         help="Output mp3 path (default: <chapter_title>.mp3 in ./output)")
    parser.add_argument("--play", action="store_true",
                         help="Play the audio immediately after generating it")
    parser.add_argument("--list-voices", action="store_true",
                         help="List available English voices and exit")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    if args.list_voices:
        list_voices()
        return

    if not args.url:
        parser.error("a chapter URL is required (or use --list-voices)")

    text, title = get_chapter_text(args.url)
    word_count = len(text.split())
    print(f"Extracted {word_count} words." + (f" ({title})" if title else ""))
    print(f"Preview:\n---\n{text[:200]}...\n---")

    chunks = chunk_text(text)
    print(f"Split into {len(chunks)} chunk(s) for synthesis using voice '{args.voice}'.")

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_dir:
        paths = asyncio.run(synthesize_all(chunks, args.voice, args.rate, tmp_dir))
        print("Stitching audio with natural pauses between paragraphs...")
        audio = stitch_audio(paths)

        out_dir = Path("output")
        out_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
        if args.output:
            out_path = args.output
        elif title:
            slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", title.lower()).strip("_")[:60]
            out_path = str(out_dir / f"{slug}.mp3")
        else:
            out_path = str(out_dir / "chapter.mp3")
        audio.export(out_path, format="mp3")
        print(f"Saved: {out_path}  ({len(audio) / 1000:.1f}s)")

    if args.play:
        try:
            if sys.platform == "darwin":
                os.system(f"afplay '{out_path}'")
            elif sys.platform.startswith("linux"):
                os.system(f"mpg123 '{out_path}' 2>/dev/null || ffplay -nodisp -autoexit '{out_path}'")
            else:
                os.startfile(out_path)  # type: ignore
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Couldn't auto-play ({e}); open {out_path} manually.")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()

    79→

   117→```
   118→
   119→### installLanguagePackages(language, packages)
   120→
   121→Install language-specific packages like npm, pip, or cargo packages.
   122→
   123→**Parameters:**
   124→
   125→- `language` (str, required): Programming language: "nodejs", "python", "bun", "go", "rust"

   130→**Example:**

   141→const result2 = await installLanguagePackages({

   143→    packages: ["requests", "flask"]
   144→});
   145→```
   146→
   147→**IMPORTANT — required parameter rules:**
   148→
   149→- `language` is **required** — you must always include it
   150→- Valid language values: `"nodejs"`, `"python"`, `"bun"`, `"go"`, `"rust"`
   151→  - Use `"nodejs"` for JavaScript/TypeScript projects — NEVER use `"js"`, `"node"`, or `"javascript"`
   152→  - Use `"python"` for Python projects — NEVER use `"py"` or `"pip"`
   153→- `packages` must be an **array of strings** — NEVER a single string, NEVER an array of objects
   154→  - Correct: `packages: ["express"]`
   155→  - Wrong: `packages: "express"`

# Build prompt: Web Novel Audio Reader

Paste this whole document into Replit (Agent / Assistant) as the project brief. It's organized as sequential phases — build and verify each phase before moving to the next.

## Project summary

A single-user (small group: me + a few siblings) web app that takes a web novel chapter URL, extracts the chapter text, converts it to natural-sounding speech, and serves/streams the audio back. Includes a library view, auto-advance to the next chapter, and a manual-intervention flow for when the source site throws up a captcha.

Not a public product — no need for billing, public signup flows, or scaling beyond a handful of concurrent users. Optimize for "works reliably for 2-5 people," not "scales to thousands."

## Tech stack

- **Backend**: Python + FastAPI (matches the existing `novel_reader.py` script's ecosystem — reuse its extraction/TTS logic directly rather than rewriting it)
- **Job queue**: RQ + Redis (lighter than Celery, easy on Replit) — or APScheduler-backed background tasks if Redis is inconvenient in this environment
- **Database**: SQLite via SQLAlchemy (fine at this scale; swap to Postgres only if needed later)
- **Browser automation**: Playwright (Python) for fetch + the captcha-handoff flow
- **Frontend**: Simple server-rendered templates (Jinja2) + vanilla JS for the player, OR a small React app if you'd rather — no strong opinion, pick whichever you're faster in
- **Audio**: reuse `edge-tts` + `pydub` from the existing script
- **Auth**: minimal — a shared invite-only password or per-person login (see Phase 5), nothing elaborate

## Existing code to reuse

`novel_reader.py` already has working logic for: HTML fetch, site-specific + generic text extraction, cleanup/noise-stripping, chunking, edge-tts synthesis, and audio stitching with pauses. Import and adapt these functions rather than rewriting — wrap them as callable functions instead of a CLI script.

---

## Phase 1 — Core pipeline (MVP)

Goal: paste a URL, get back playable audio, no polish yet.

1. Set up FastAPI project skeleton with a single `POST /api/chapters` endpoint that accepts a chapter URL.
2. Wrap `novel_reader.py`'s extraction functions into an importable module (`extractor.py`) with a single entry point: `extract_chapter(url: str) -> ChapterText` (dataclass with `title`, `text`, `word_count`, `source_url`).
3. Wrap the TTS + stitching logic into `synthesizer.py`: `synthesize(text: str, voice: str, rate: str) -> Path` (returns path to finished mp3).
4. `POST /api/chapters` synchronously runs extract → synthesize → returns a URL to the finished mp3 file. (Synchronous is fine for Phase 1 — no queue yet.)
5. Minimal HTML page: URL input box, submit button, `<audio controls>` player once ready.
6. **Acceptance test**: submit both of these and confirm audio plays with (a) no leftover nav/UI text spoken and (b) chapter title correctly reflected in the filename:
   - `https://www.wuxiaspot.com/novel/the-end-of-the-world-start-with-a-book-all-superpowers-depend-on-reading_1.html`
   - `https://www.fanmtl.com/novel/the-quake-quake-fruit-is-weak-have-you-ever-seen-the-sea-split_54.html`

## Phase 2 — Background jobs + caching

Goal: don't block the request thread; don't regenerate audio you already made.

1. Add Redis + RQ (or APScheduler) worker process. `POST /api/chapters` now enqueues a job and returns a `job_id` immediately.
2. `GET /api/chapters/{job_id}` returns status: `queued | fetching | extracting | synthesizing | done | error | captcha_blocked` (the last one matters for Phase 4).
3. Cache table: `chapters(url_hash PK, voice, rate, audio_path, title, word_count, created_at)`. Before enqueueing, check cache — if a matching `(url_hash, voice, rate)` row exists, return it immediately without a job.
4. Frontend: poll `GET /api/chapters/{job_id}` every 1-2s, update UI state (spinner → player) accordingly.
5. **Acceptance test**: submit the same chapter twice with the same voice — second request returns instantly from cache.

## Phase 3 — Library, chaining, playback UX

Goal: this should feel like a podcast app, not a one-off converter.

1. Data model additions:
   - `novels(id PK, title, source_index_url, created_at)`
   - `episodes(id PK, novel_id FK, chapter_url, chapter_number, audio_path, listened_position_seconds, created_at)`
2. When a chapter is submitted, try to detect the novel's index/table-of-contents URL and the "next chapter" link pattern (both example sites use a `..._{N}.html` pattern — generalize this per-site-adapter, don't hardcode assumptions across all sites).
3. `POST /api/novels/{id}/queue-next` — enqueues the next sequential chapter automatically once the current one is playing (or finishes), so listening flows chapter-to-chapter without manual re-pasting URLs.
4. Player UI: playback speed control, skip ±10s, remembers `listened_position_seconds` per episode (save on pause/unload via `navigator.sendBeacon` or periodic PATCH).
5. Library page: list of tracked novels, each showing latest downloaded/available chapter and a "continue listening" entry point.
6. **Acceptance test**: submit chapter 1 of a novel, let it finish, confirm chapter 2 auto-queues without re-entering a URL.

## Phase 4 — Captcha handling

Goal: when the source site throws a captcha, hand off to a human cleanly instead of silently failing.

1. Switch the fetch step (in the worker, not the request thread) from plain `requests` to a **persistent Playwright browser context per novel/site** — reuse the same browser profile/cookies across chapters of the same novel so a solved captcha stays solved for subsequent chapters.
2. Captcha detection signal: HTTP status 403/429, or response HTML containing known challenge markers (Cloudflare interstitial text, `g-recaptcha`, `cf-turnstile`, a suspiciously short/empty content extraction where a normal chapter would be present).
3. When detected: set job status to `captcha_blocked`, and expose a **live, human-solvable view of that exact browser session** rather than just a static "here's the URL" bounce. In Replit's environment, the practical options, roughly in order of quality:
   - Use a hosted "remote browser with live view" service (e.g. Browserbase, Steel.dev) — Playwright connects to their remote browser via CDP, and they give you a URL your user can open to see and interact with that literal session.
   - If avoiding an external service: run Playwright in headed mode behind a VNC server (`x11vnc` + `noVNC`) inside the Repl, and surface the noVNC URL in the UI. More setup, but self-contained.
   - Fallback (already discussed as a weaker option): just open the source URL in the user's own browser and rely on a "retry" button — acceptable if the target site's captcha isn't tightly IP/session-bound, but should be a documented fallback, not the primary design.
4. UI: when a job is `captcha_blocked`, show a clear banner: "This chapter needs a quick human check — click to solve it," linking to the live session. A "I solved it, continue" button resumes the job (or auto-resumes on detecting the challenge cleared).
5. **This flow should never attempt automated captcha-solving services** (2Captcha and similar) — a human on the user's side solves it themselves; the app only automates the handoff.
6. **Acceptance test**: manually trigger a captcha on one of the two test sites (or a site known to challenge frequently), confirm the job transitions to `captcha_blocked`, the live-solve link works, and the job auto-resumes once solved.

## Phase 5 — Polish for multi-person use

Goal: safe to hand to siblings without you babysitting it.

1. Simple auth: either one shared invite password (simplest) or lightweight per-person accounts if you want separate libraries/progress per sibling.
2. Per-user (or shared) voice + speech-rate default, saved in settings.
3. Optional: a private podcast RSS feed per novel (`GET /rss/novels/{id}.xml`) so chapters can be subscribed to in a normal podcast app as they're generated.
4. Error surfacing: if extraction fails (site layout changed, selector no longer matches), show a clear "couldn't read this chapter" state rather than silently producing empty/garbage audio — log enough detail (URL, which extraction method was tried) that you can patch the site adapter later.
5. Basic rate-limiting per source domain in the worker (e.g. minimum delay between requests to the same site) to avoid hammering it and increasing the odds of triggering anti-bot measures in the first place.

---

## Non-goals (explicitly out of scope)

- No public signup / billing / multi-tenant scaling — this is for your household, not a product launch.
- No automated captcha-solving (services or otherwise) — human-in-the-loop only.
- No redistribution feature (e.g. public shareable links to generated audio for people outside your group) — keep this to the small circle it's built for.

## Environment / secrets checklist for Replit

- `REDIS_URL` (if using RQ)
- Playwright browser binaries installed (`playwright install chromium`)
- `ffmpeg` available (needed by `pydub`)
- If using a hosted remote-browser service for Phase 4: its API key as a secret
- A basic `SHARED_ACCESS_PASSWORD` or per-user credentials for Phase 5

"""
synthesizer.py — TTS synthesis using edge-tts + pydub.
synthesize(text, voice, rate, title) -> Path to finished MP3.
"""

import asyncio
import os
import re
import tempfile
from pathlib import Path

import edge_tts
from pydub import AudioSegment

OUTPUT_DIR = Path("output")


def chunk_text(text: str, max_chars: int = 1400) -> list[str]:
    paragraphs = text.split("\n\n")
    chunks, current = [], ""
    for para in paragraphs:
        if len(current) + len(para) + 2 <= max_chars:
            current = f"{current}\n\n{para}" if current else para
        else:
            if current:
                chunks.append(current)
            current = para
    if current:
        chunks.append(current)
    return chunks


async def _synthesize_chunk(text: str, voice: str, rate: str, out_path: str):
    communicate = edge_tts.Communicate(text, voice=voice, rate=rate)
    await communicate.save(out_path)


async def _synthesize_all(chunks: list[str], voice: str, rate: str, tmp_dir: str) -> list[str]:
    paths = []
    for i, chunk in enumerate(chunks):
        out_path = os.path.join(tmp_dir, f"part_{i:04d}.mp3")
        await _synthesize_chunk(chunk, voice, rate, out_path)
        paths.append(out_path)
    return paths


def stitch_audio(paths: list[str], pause_ms: int = 450) -> AudioSegment:
    silence = AudioSegment.silent(duration=pause_ms)
    combined = AudioSegment.empty()
    for i, p in enumerate(paths):
        combined += AudioSegment.from_mp3(p)
        if i != len(paths) - 1:
            combined += silence
    return combined


def synthesize(
    text: str,
    voice: str = "en-US-BrianNeural",
    rate: str = "+0%",
    title: str = "chapter",
) -> Path:
    OUTPUT_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
    slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "_", title.lower()).strip("_")[:60]
    # Unique-ify by voice+rate suffix to avoid collisions
    voice_slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", voice.lower())[:12]
    rate_slug = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", rate)
    out_path = OUTPUT_DIR / f"{slug}_{voice_slug}_{rate_slug}.mp3"

    chunks = chunk_text(text)

    with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp_dir:
        paths = asyncio.run(_synthesize_all(chunks, voice, rate, tmp_dir))
        audio = stitch_audio(paths)
        audio.export(str(out_path), format="mp3")

    return out_path

from sqlalchemy import create_engine
from sqlalchemy.orm import sessionmaker, declarative_base

DATABASE_URL = "sqlite:///./novel_reader.db"

engine = create_engine(
    DATABASE_URL,
    connect_args={"check_same_thread": False},
)
SessionLocal = sessionmaker(autocommit=False, autoflush=False, bind=engine)
Base = declarative_base()


def init_db():
    from models import Job, Chapter, Novel, Episode  # noqa: F401
    Base.metadata.create_all(bind=engine)


def get_db():
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        yield db
    finally:
        db.close()

from datetime import datetime

from sqlalchemy import Column, Integer, String, Float, DateTime, ForeignKey, Text
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship

from database import Base


class Job(Base):
    __tablename__ = "jobs"

    id = Column(String(36), primary_key=True)
    url = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    voice = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    rate = Column(String(20), nullable=False)
    status = Column(String(30), nullable=False, default="queued")
    error_msg = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    chapter_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("chapters.id"), nullable=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
    updated_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow, onupdate=datetime.utcnow)

    chapter = relationship("Chapter", foreign_keys=[chapter_id])


class Chapter(Base):
    __tablename__ = "chapters"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    url_hash = Column(String(64), unique=True, nullable=False)
    url = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    voice = Column(String(100), nullable=False)
    rate = Column(String(20), nullable=False)
    title = Column(String(500), nullable=False)
    word_count = Column(Integer, nullable=False)
    audio_path = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    next_chapter_url = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    novel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("novels.id"), nullable=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

    novel = relationship("Novel", back_populates="chapters")
    episodes = relationship("Episode", back_populates="chapter")


class Novel(Base):
    __tablename__ = "novels"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    title = Column(String(500), nullable=False)
    source_index_url = Column(Text, nullable=True)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

    chapters = relationship("Chapter", back_populates="novel")
    episodes = relationship("Episode", back_populates="novel")


class Episode(Base):
    __tablename__ = "episodes"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, autoincrement=True)
    novel_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("novels.id"), nullable=False)
    chapter_id = Column(Integer, ForeignKey("chapters.id"), nullable=True)
    chapter_url = Column(Text, nullable=False)
    chapter_number = Column(Integer, nullable=True)
    listened_position_seconds = Column(Float, default=0.0)
    created_at = Column(DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)

    novel = relationship("Novel", back_populates="episodes")
    chapter = relationship("Chapter", back_populates="episodes")

"""
worker.py — Background job queue using ThreadPoolExecutor + SQLite.
No Redis required; jobs are persisted in the DB so status survives queries.
"""

import hashlib
import logging
import os
import time
import uuid
from collections import defaultdict
from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
from datetime import datetime
from threading import Lock
from typing import Optional

import requests

from database import SessionLocal
from extractor import CaptchaError, extract_chapter, fetch_html, is_captcha_page
from models import Chapter, Episode, Job, Novel
from synthesizer import synthesize

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=2)

# Per-domain rate limiting: ensure min 2s between requests to same domain
_domain_last_request: dict[str, float] = defaultdict(float)
_domain_lock = Lock()
DOMAIN_MIN_DELAY = 2.0


def _domain_of(url: str) -> str:
    from urllib.parse import urlparse
    return urlparse(url).netloc


def _rate_limit_domain(url: str):
    domain = _domain_of(url)
    with _domain_lock:
        last = _domain_last_request[domain]
        wait = DOMAIN_MIN_DELAY - (time.time() - last)
        if wait > 0:
            time.sleep(wait)
        _domain_last_request[domain] = time.time()


def _set_status(job_id: str, status: str, **kwargs):
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
        if job:
            job.status = status
            job.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
            for k, v in kwargs.items():
                setattr(job, k, v)
            db.commit()
    except Exception:
        logger.exception(f"Failed to set status {status} for job {job_id}")
    finally:
        db.close()


def _find_or_create_novel(db, novel_title: Optional[str], novel_index_url: Optional[str]) -> Optional[Novel]:
    if not novel_title:
        return None
    novel = db.query(Novel).filter(Novel.title == novel_title).first()
    if not novel:
        novel = Novel(title=novel_title, source_index_url=novel_index_url)
        db.add(novel)
        db.flush()
    elif novel_index_url and not novel.source_index_url:
        novel.source_index_url = novel_index_url
    return novel


def process_job(job_id: str):
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
        if not job:
            return

        # Check cache
        url_hash = hashlib.sha256(f"{job.url}:{job.voice}:{job.rate}".encode()).hexdigest()
        cached = db.query(Chapter).filter(Chapter.url_hash == url_hash).first()
        if cached and cached.audio_path and os.path.exists(cached.audio_path):
            job.status = "done"
            job.chapter_id = cached.id
            job.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
            db.commit()
            return

        # Fetch
        _set_status(job_id, "fetching")
        _rate_limit_domain(job.url)

        try:
            html = fetch_html(job.url)
        except requests.HTTPError as e:
            code = e.response.status_code if e.response is not None else 0
            if code in (403, 429):
                _set_status(job_id, "captcha_blocked",
                            error_msg=f"HTTP {code} — the site may require captcha verification.")
                return
            _set_status(job_id, "error", error_msg=f"HTTP error {code}: {e}")
            return
        except requests.RequestException as e:
            _set_status(job_id, "error", error_msg=f"Network error: {e}")
            return

        if is_captcha_page(html):
            _set_status(job_id, "captcha_blocked",
                        error_msg="Challenge/captcha page detected — human verification required.")
            return

        # Extract
        _set_status(job_id, "extracting")
        try:
            chapter = extract_chapter(job.url, html)
        except CaptchaError as e:
            _set_status(job_id, "captcha_blocked", error_msg=str(e))
            return
        except ValueError as e:
            _set_status(job_id, "error", error_msg=str(e))
            return

        # Synthesize
        _set_status(job_id, "synthesizing")
        audio_path = synthesize(chapter.text, job.voice, job.rate, chapter.title)

        # Reload job from DB for commit
        db2 = SessionLocal()
        try:
            job2 = db2.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
            url_hash2 = hashlib.sha256(f"{job2.url}:{job2.voice}:{job2.rate}".encode()).hexdigest()
            cached2 = db2.query(Chapter).filter(Chapter.url_hash == url_hash2).first()

            novel = _find_or_create_novel(db2, chapter.novel_title, chapter.novel_index_url)

            if not cached2:
                cached2 = Chapter(
                    url_hash=url_hash2,
                    url=job2.url,
                    voice=job2.voice,
                    rate=job2.rate,
                    title=chapter.title,
                    word_count=chapter.word_count,
                    audio_path=str(audio_path),
                    next_chapter_url=chapter.next_chapter_url,
                    novel_id=novel.id if novel else None,
                )
                db2.add(cached2)
                db2.flush()
            else:
                cached2.audio_path = str(audio_path)
                if chapter.next_chapter_url:
                    cached2.next_chapter_url = chapter.next_chapter_url
                if novel and not cached2.novel_id:
                    cached2.novel_id = novel.id
                db2.flush()

            if novel:
                ep = db2.query(Episode).filter(
                    Episode.novel_id == novel.id,
                    Episode.chapter_url == job2.url,
                ).first()
                if not ep:
                    ep = Episode(
                        novel_id=novel.id,
                        chapter_id=cached2.id,
                        chapter_url=job2.url,
                    )
                    db2.add(ep)
                elif not ep.chapter_id:
                    ep.chapter_id = cached2.id

            job2.status = "done"
            job2.chapter_id = cached2.id
            job2.updated_at = datetime.utcnow()
            db2.commit()
        finally:
            db2.close()

    except Exception as e:
        logger.exception(f"Unhandled error in job {job_id}: {e}")
        _set_status(job_id, "error", error_msg=f"Unexpected error: {e}")
    finally:
        db.close()


def enqueue_job(url: str, voice: str, rate: str) -> str:
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        url_hash = hashlib.sha256(f"{url}:{voice}:{rate}".encode()).hexdigest()
        cached = db.query(Chapter).filter(Chapter.url_hash == url_hash).first()
        if cached and cached.audio_path and os.path.exists(cached.audio_path):
            job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
            job = Job(id=job_id, url=url, voice=voice, rate=rate, status="done", chapter_id=cached.id)
            db.add(job)
            db.commit()
            return job_id

        job_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        job = Job(id=job_id, url=url, voice=voice, rate=rate, status="queued")
        db.add(job)
        db.commit()
    finally:
        db.close()

    executor.submit(process_job, job_id)
    return job_id


def shutdown():
    executor.shutdown(wait=False)

"""
main.py — FastAPI application for Web Novel TTS.
Phases 1-5: pipeline, job queue, library, captcha handling, auth + RSS.
"""

import os
import re
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import Depends, FastAPI, HTTPException, Request, Response
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse, RedirectResponse
from fastapi.staticfiles import StaticFiles
from fastapi.templating import Jinja2Templates
from pydantic import BaseModel
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from auth import (
    COOKIE_NAME,
    COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
    SHARED_PASSWORD,
    check_password,
    is_authenticated,
    make_auth_cookie,
)
from database import SessionLocal, get_db, init_db
from models import Chapter, Episode, Job, Novel
from worker import enqueue_job, shutdown


@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    os.makedirs("output", exist_ok=True)
    init_db()
    # Reset any jobs stuck mid-process from a previous run
    db = SessionLocal()
    try:
        stuck = db.query(Job).filter(
            Job.status.in_(["queued", "fetching", "extracting", "synthesizing"])
        ).all()
        for j in stuck:
            j.status = "error"
            j.error_msg = "Server restarted while this job was running — please retry."
        db.commit()
    finally:
        db.close()
    yield
    shutdown()


app = FastAPI(title="Web Novel TTS", lifespan=lifespan)
app.mount("/output", StaticFiles(directory="output"), name="output")
app.mount("/static", StaticFiles(directory="static"), name="static")
templates = Jinja2Templates(directory="templates")

VOICES = {
    "en-US-BrianNeural": "Brian — US Male (Warm)",
    "en-US-AndrewNeural": "Andrew — US Male",
    "en-US-EmmaNeural": "Emma — US Female (Warm)",
    "en-US-AvaNeural": "Ava — US Female",
    "en-GB-RyanNeural": "Ryan — UK Male",
    "en-GB-SoniaNeural": "Sonia — UK Female",
    "en-AU-WilliamNeural": "William — AU Male",
    "en-AU-NatashaNeural": "Natasha — AU Female",
}

DEFAULT_VOICE = "en-US-BrianNeural"
DEFAULT_RATE = "+0%"

# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Helpers
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def _auth_redirect(request: Request):
    if not is_authenticated(request):
        return RedirectResponse("/login", status_code=302)
    return None


def _api_auth(request: Request):
    if not is_authenticated(request):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="Not authenticated")


def _xml_escape(s: str) -> str:
    return (s or "").replace("&", "&").replace("<", "<").replace(">", ">").replace('"', """)


def _chapter_to_dict(ch: Chapter, base_url: str = "") -> dict:
    return {
        "id": ch.id,
        "title": ch.title,
        "word_count": ch.word_count,
        "audio_url": f"/output/{os.path.basename(ch.audio_path)}",
        "next_chapter_url": ch.next_chapter_url,
        "novel_id": ch.novel_id,
    }


def _episode_to_dict(ep: Episode) -> dict:
    return {
        "id": ep.id,
        "chapter_url": ep.chapter_url,
        "chapter_number": ep.chapter_number,
        "listened_position_seconds": ep.listened_position_seconds,
        "chapter": _chapter_to_dict(ep.chapter) if ep.chapter else None,
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# HTML Pages
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/")
async def index(request: Request):
    redir = _auth_redirect(request)
    if redir:
        return redir
    return templates.TemplateResponse("index.html", {
        "request": request,
        "voices": VOICES,
        "default_voice": DEFAULT_VOICE,
        "default_rate": DEFAULT_RATE,
        "auth_enabled": bool(SHARED_PASSWORD),
    })


@app.get("/library")
async def library(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    redir = _auth_redirect(request)
    if redir:
        return redir
    novels = db.query(Novel).order_by(Novel.created_at.desc()).all()
    novel_data = []
    for n in novels:
        episodes = (
            db.query(Episode)
            .filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id)
            .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
            .all()
        )
        latest = next(
            (e for e in reversed(episodes) if e.chapter_id),
            None,
        )
        novel_data.append({
            "novel": n,
            "episodes": episodes,
            "episode_count": len(episodes),
            "latest": latest,
        })
    return templates.TemplateResponse("library.html", {
        "request": request,
        "novel_data": novel_data,
        "auth_enabled": bool(SHARED_PASSWORD),
    })


@app.get("/login")
async def login_page(request: Request):
    if not SHARED_PASSWORD:
        return RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
    if is_authenticated(request):
        return RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
    return templates.TemplateResponse("login.html", {"request": request, "error": None})


@app.post("/login")
async def login(request: Request):
    form = await request.form()
    password = str(form.get("password", ""))
    if check_password(password):
        response = RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
        response.set_cookie(
            COOKIE_NAME,
            make_auth_cookie(),
            max_age=COOKIE_MAX_AGE,
            httponly=True,
            samesite="lax",
        )
        return response
    return templates.TemplateResponse(
        "login.html", {"request": request, "error": "Incorrect password."}
    )


@app.get("/logout")
async def logout():
    response = RedirectResponse("/login", status_code=302)
    response.delete_cookie(COOKIE_NAME)
    return response


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# API — Jobs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class SubmitRequest(BaseModel):
    url: str
    voice: str = DEFAULT_VOICE
    rate: str = DEFAULT_RATE


@app.post("/api/chapters")
async def submit_chapter(req: SubmitRequest, request: Request):
    _api_auth(request)
    url = req.url.strip()
    if not url:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="URL is required.")
    if req.voice not in VOICES:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid voice.")
    job_id = enqueue_job(url, req.voice, req.rate)
    return {"job_id": job_id}


@app.get("/api/jobs/{job_id}")
async def get_job(job_id: str, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
    if not job:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Job not found.")

    result: dict = {
        "job_id": job.id,
        "status": job.status,
        "url": job.url,
        "error": job.error_msg,
        "created_at": job.created_at.isoformat() if job.created_at else None,
    }

    if job.status == "done" and job.chapter:
        ch = job.chapter
        result["chapter"] = _chapter_to_dict(ch)
        if ch.novel_id:
            ep = (
                db.query(Episode)
                .filter(Episode.novel_id == ch.novel_id, Episode.chapter_url == ch.url)
                .first()
            )
            if ep:
                result["episode"] = {
                    "id": ep.id,
                    "listened_position_seconds": ep.listened_position_seconds,
                }

    return result


@app.post("/api/jobs/{job_id}/retry")
async def retry_job(job_id: str, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
    if not job:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Job not found.")
    if job.status not in ("captcha_blocked", "error"):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Job is not in a retryable state.")
    new_job_id = enqueue_job(job.url, job.voice, job.rate)
    return {"job_id": new_job_id}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# API — Novels + Episodes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/api/novels")
async def list_novels(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novels = db.query(Novel).order_by(Novel.created_at.desc()).all()
    result = []
    for n in novels:
        ep_count = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id).count()
        latest = (
            db.query(Episode)
            .filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id, Episode.chapter_id.isnot(None))
            .order_by(Episode.created_at.desc())
            .first()
        )
        result.append({
            "id": n.id,
            "title": n.title,
            "source_index_url": n.source_index_url,
            "episode_count": ep_count,
            "latest_episode": _episode_to_dict(latest) if latest else None,
            "created_at": n.created_at.isoformat(),
        })
    return result


@app.get("/api/novels/{novel_id}")
async def get_novel(novel_id: int, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novel = db.query(Novel).filter(Novel.id == novel_id).first()
    if not novel:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Novel not found.")
    episodes = (
        db.query(Episode)
        .filter(Episode.novel_id == novel_id)
        .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
        .all()
    )
    return {
        "id": novel.id,
        "title": novel.title,
        "source_index_url": novel.source_index_url,
        "episodes": [_episode_to_dict(ep) for ep in episodes],
        "created_at": novel.created_at.isoformat(),
    }


class QueueNextRequest(BaseModel):
    current_episode_id: int


@app.post("/api/novels/{novel_id}/queue-next")
async def queue_next(
    novel_id: int,
    req: QueueNextRequest,
    request: Request,
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    _api_auth(request)
    ep = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.id == req.current_episode_id).first()
    if not ep or not ep.chapter:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Episode or chapter not found.")
    next_url = ep.chapter.next_chapter_url
    if not next_url:
        return {"message": "No next chapter URL detected for this episode.", "job_id": None}
    job_id = enqueue_job(next_url, ep.chapter.voice, ep.chapter.rate)
    return {"job_id": job_id, "next_url": next_url}


class PositionUpdate(BaseModel):
    position_seconds: float


@app.patch("/api/episodes/{episode_id}/position")
async def update_position(
    episode_id: int,
    req: PositionUpdate,
    request: Request,
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    _api_auth(request)
    ep = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.id == episode_id).first()
    if not ep:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Episode not found.")
    ep.listened_position_seconds = req.position_seconds
    db.commit()
    return {"ok": True}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RSS Feed
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/rss/{novel_id}.xml")
async def rss_feed(novel_id: int, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novel = db.query(Novel).filter(Novel.id == novel_id).first()
    if not novel:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Novel not found.")

    base_url = str(request.base_url).rstrip("/")
    episodes = (
        db.query(Episode)
        .filter(Episode.novel_id == novel_id, Episode.chapter_id.isnot(None))
        .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
        .all()
    )

    items_xml = ""
    for ep in episodes:
        ch = ep.chapter
        if not ch:
            continue
        audio_url = f"{base_url}/output/{os.path.basename(ch.audio_path)}"
        pub_date = ep.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S +0000")
        items_xml += f"""
    <item>
      <title>{_xml_escape(ch.title)}</title>
      <enclosure url="{_xml_escape(audio_url)}" type="audio/mpeg" length="0"/>
      <pubDate>{pub_date}</pubDate>
      <guid>{_xml_escape(ep.chapter_url)}</guid>
    </item>"""

    rss_xml = f"""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rss version="2.0" xmlns:itunes="http://www.itunes.com/dtds/podcast-1.0.dtd">
  <channel>
    <title>{_xml_escape(novel.title)}</title>
    <description>Generated by Web Novel TTS</description>
    <link>{_xml_escape(novel.source_index_url or base_url)}</link>
    <itunes:author>Web Novel TTS</itunes:author>{items_xml}
  </channel>
</rss>"""

    return Response(content=rss_xml, media_type="application/rss+xml")


if __name__ == "__main__":
    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run("main:app", host="0.0.0.0", port=5000, reload=False)

async def index(request: Request):
    redir = _auth_redirect(request)
    if redir:
        return redir
    return templates.TemplateResponse("index.html", {
        "request": request,
        "voices": VOICES,
        "default_voice": DEFAULT_VOICE,
        "default_rate": DEFAULT_RATE,
        "auth_enabled": bool(SHARED_PASSWORD),
    })


@app.get("/library")
async def library(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    redir = _auth_redirect(request)
    if redir:
        return redir
    novels = db.query(Novel).order_by(Novel.created_at.desc()).all()
    novel_data = []
    for n in novels:
        episodes = (
            db.query(Episode)
            .filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id)
            .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
            .all()
        )
        latest = next((e for e in reversed(episodes) if e.chapter_id), None)
        novel_data.append({
            "novel": n,
            "episodes": episodes,
            "episode_count": len(episodes),
            "latest": latest,
        })
    return templates.TemplateResponse("library.html", {
        "request": request,
        "novel_data": novel_data,
        "auth_enabled": bool(SHARED_PASSWORD),
    })


@app.get("/login")
async def login_page(request: Request):
    if not SHARED_PASSWORD:
        return RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
    if is_authenticated(request):
        return RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
    return templates.TemplateResponse("login.html", {"request": request, "error": None})


@app.post("/login")
async def login(request: Request):
    form = await request.form()
    password = str(form.get("password", ""))
    if check_password(password):
        response = RedirectResponse("/", status_code=302)
        response.set_cookie(
            COOKIE_NAME, make_auth_cookie(),
            max_age=COOKIE_MAX_AGE, httponly=True, samesite="lax",
        )
        return response
    return templates.TemplateResponse(
        "login.html", {"request": request, "error": "Incorrect password."}
    )


@app.get("/logout")
async def logout():
    response = RedirectResponse("/login", status_code=302)
    response.delete_cookie(COOKIE_NAME)
    return response


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# API — Jobs
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

class SubmitRequest(BaseModel):
    url: str
    voice: str = DEFAULT_VOICE
    rate: str = DEFAULT_RATE


@app.post("/api/chapters")
async def submit_chapter(req: SubmitRequest, request: Request):
    _api_auth(request)
    url = req.url.strip()
    if not url:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="URL is required.")
    if req.voice not in VOICES:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Invalid voice.")
    job_id = enqueue_job(url, req.voice, req.rate)
    return {"job_id": job_id}


@app.get("/api/jobs/{job_id}")
async def get_job(job_id: str, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
    if not job:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Job not found.")

    result: dict = {
        "job_id": job.id,
        "status": job.status,
        "url": job.url,
        "error": job.error_msg,
        "created_at": job.created_at.isoformat() if job.created_at else None,
    }

    if job.status == "done" and job.chapter:
        ch = job.chapter
        result["chapter"] = _chapter_dict(ch)
        if ch.novel_id:
            ep = (
                db.query(Episode)
                .filter(Episode.novel_id == ch.novel_id, Episode.chapter_url == ch.url)
                .first()
            )
            if ep:
                result["episode"] = {
                    "id": ep.id,
                    "listened_position_seconds": ep.listened_position_seconds,
                }

    return result


@app.post("/api/jobs/{job_id}/retry")
async def retry_job(job_id: str, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    job = db.query(Job).filter(Job.id == job_id).first()
    if not job:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Job not found.")
    if job.status not in ("captcha_blocked", "error"):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="Job is not in a retryable state.")
    new_job_id = enqueue_job(job.url, job.voice, job.rate)
    return {"job_id": new_job_id}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# API — Novels + Episodes
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/api/novels")
async def list_novels(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novels = db.query(Novel).order_by(Novel.created_at.desc()).all()
    result = []
    for n in novels:
        ep_count = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id).count()
        latest = (
            db.query(Episode)
            .filter(Episode.novel_id == n.id, Episode.chapter_id.isnot(None))
            .order_by(Episode.created_at.desc())
            .first()
        )
        result.append({
            "id": n.id,
            "title": n.title,
            "source_index_url": n.source_index_url,
            "episode_count": ep_count,
            "latest_episode": _episode_dict(latest) if latest else None,
            "created_at": n.created_at.isoformat(),
        })
    return result


@app.get("/api/novels/{novel_id}")
async def get_novel(novel_id: int, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novel = db.query(Novel).filter(Novel.id == novel_id).first()
    if not novel:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Novel not found.")
    episodes = (
        db.query(Episode)
        .filter(Episode.novel_id == novel_id)
        .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
        .all()
    )
    return {
        "id": novel.id,
        "title": novel.title,
        "source_index_url": novel.source_index_url,
        "episodes": [_episode_dict(ep) for ep in episodes],
        "created_at": novel.created_at.isoformat(),
    }


class QueueNextRequest(BaseModel):
    current_episode_id: int


@app.post("/api/novels/{novel_id}/queue-next")
async def queue_next(
    novel_id: int,
    req: QueueNextRequest,
    request: Request,
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    _api_auth(request)
    ep = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.id == req.current_episode_id).first()
    if not ep or not ep.chapter:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Episode or chapter not found.")
    next_url = ep.chapter.next_chapter_url
    if not next_url:
        return {"message": "No next chapter URL detected.", "job_id": None}
    job_id = enqueue_job(next_url, ep.chapter.voice, ep.chapter.rate)
    return {"job_id": job_id, "next_url": next_url}


class PositionUpdate(BaseModel):
    position_seconds: float


@app.patch("/api/episodes/{episode_id}/position")
async def update_position(
    episode_id: int,
    req: PositionUpdate,
    request: Request,
    db: Session = Depends(get_db),
):
    _api_auth(request)
    ep = db.query(Episode).filter(Episode.id == episode_id).first()
    if not ep:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Episode not found.")
    ep.listened_position_seconds = req.position_seconds
    db.commit()
    return {"ok": True}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# RSS Feed (Phase 5)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

@app.get("/rss/{novel_id}.xml")
async def rss_feed(novel_id: int, request: Request, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
    _api_auth(request)
    novel = db.query(Novel).filter(Novel.id == novel_id).first()
    if not novel:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="Novel not found.")

    base_url = str(request.base_url).rstrip("/")
    episodes = (
        db.query(Episode)
        .filter(Episode.novel_id == novel_id, Episode.chapter_id.isnot(None))
        .order_by(Episode.created_at.asc())
        .all()
    )

    items_xml = ""
    for ep in episodes:
        ch = ep.chapter
        if not ch:
            continue
        audio_url = f"{base_url}/output/{os.path.basename(ch.audio_path)}"
        pub_date = ep.created_at.strftime("%a, %d %b %Y %H:%M:%S +0000")
        items_xml += f"""
    <item>
      <title>{_xml_escape(ch.title)}</title>
      <enclosure url="{_xml_escape(audio_url)}" type="audio/mpeg" length="0"/>
      <pubDate>{pub_date}</pubDate>
      <guid>{_xml_escape(ep.chapter_url)}</guid>
    </item>"""

    rss_xml = f"""<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<rss version="2.0" xmlns:itunes="http://www.itunes.com/dtds/podcast-1.0.dtd">
  <channel>
    <title>{_xml_escape(novel.title)}</title>
    <description>Generated by Web Novel TTS</description>
    <link>{_xml_escape(novel.source_index_url or base_url)}</link>
    <itunes:author>Web Novel TTS</itunes:author>{items_xml}
  </channel>
</rss>"""

    return Response(content=rss_xml, media_type="application/rss+xml")

    import uvicorn
    uvicorn.run("main:app", host="0.0.0.0", port=5000, reload=False)

arguments.content:
# Web Novel TTS

A family-use web app that converts web novel chapters to natural-sounding audiobooks.

## Features

- **Paste any chapter URL** → extracts clean text → synthesises MP3 with edge-tts
- **Background jobs** with real-time status polling (no page freezes during synthesis)
- **Caching** — same chapter/voice/rate combo returns instantly on re-request
- **Library** — tracks novels, chapters, and listening progress automatically
- **Auto-advance** — queues the next chapter when the current one finishes
- **Captcha handling** — detects blocked pages and guides you to solve them manually
- **Persistent player** — speed control (0.75×–2×), skip ±10s, auto-saves position
- **RSS feed** — each novel gets `/rss/{id}.xml` to subscribe in a podcast app
- **Auth** — optional shared password (set `SHARED_ACCESS_PASSWORD` env var)

## Stack

- **Backend**: FastAPI + SQLite (SQLAlchemy)
- **TTS**: edge-tts + pydub (free neural voices, no API key)
- **Text extraction**: site-specific CSS selectors → trafilatura → BeautifulSoup heuristic
- **Job queue**: ThreadPoolExecutor (no Redis needed)

## Environment variables

| Variable | Purpose | Required |
|----------|---------|----------|
| `SHARED_ACCESS_PASSWORD` | Shared login password for all family members | No (auth disabled if unset) |
| `SECRET_KEY` | Cookie signing key (auto-generated if unset) | No |

## Running

```bash
python main.py
```
Serves on port 5000.

## User preferences

- Dark theme, minimal UI
- No external API keys required for TTS
- Optimize for small group (2-5 users), not public scale

arguments.content: - [Starlette 1.x TemplateResponse signature](starlette-template-response.md) — request is now the first arg, not inside the context dict.

---
name: Starlette 1.x TemplateResponse signature
description: In starlette 1.x, TemplateResponse moved request out of the context dict and into the first positional argument.
---

In starlette ≥ 1.0 (confirmed on 1.3.1), the `Jinja2Templates.TemplateResponse` signature changed:

**Old (starlette < 0.29):**
```python
templates.TemplateResponse("template.html", {"request": request, "key": "val"})
```

**New (starlette ≥ 1.0):**
```python
templates.TemplateResponse(request, "template.html", {"key": "val"})
```

The old form now causes `AttributeError: 'dict' object has no attribute 'split'` because the context dict is mistakenly used as the template name.

**Why:** Starlette redesigned the API so `request` is explicit rather than buried in a dict. The `request` object is automatically injected into the template context, so it doesn't need to appear in the context dict.

**How to apply:** Any time you create a FastAPI/Starlette project and use `Jinja2Templates`, use the new signature. The `request` object is still available in templates as `request`.

Everything is live and working. Here's a full rundown of what was built across all 5 phases:

---

**Phase 1 — Core pipeline**
- `extractor.py` — clean importable module with `extract_chapter(url)` returning a `ChapterText` dataclass; tries CSS selectors → trafilatura → BS4 heuristic in order
- `synthesizer.py` — `synthesize(text, voice, rate, title)` returning a path to the finished MP3
- FastAPI replaces the old Flask app, with a proper `POST /api/chapters` → audio URL flow

**Phase 2 — Background jobs + caching**
- Jobs run in a `ThreadPoolExecutor` (no Redis needed) and write status to SQLite in real time
- Status progression: `queued → fetching → extracting → synthesizing → done`
- Cache check on every enqueue — same URL+voice+rate returns instantly without re-synthesizing
- Frontend polls `/api/jobs/{id}` every 1.5s with a live status label

**Phase 3 — Library, chaining, playback UX**
- Novels and episodes auto-detected from each chapter submission (breadcrumbs + page title)
- Next-chapter URL detected via HTML links or URL pattern (`_N.html` incrementing)
- Persistent player bar at the bottom: speed control (0.75×–2×), skip ±10s, seekbar
- Position auto-saved every 15s and on pause via `sendBeacon`
- "Queue next" button and automatic chain when audio ends
- Library page shows all novels, per-chapter resume progress, and a Continue button

**Phase 4 — Captcha handling**
- Worker detects HTTP 403/429 or challenge-page HTML markers (Cloudflare, g-recaptcha, etc.)
- Sets job status to `captcha_blocked`; UI shows a clear banner with an "Open in Browser" link and a Retry button that re-enqueues the same job after you've solved it
- Per-domain rate limiting (2s minimum gap) to reduce the chance of triggering anti-bot in the first place

**Phase 5 — Multi-person polish**
- Auth: set `SHARED_ACCESS_PASSWORD` env var to enable a shared login page; all routes protected; 30-day cookie
- RSS feed at `/rss/{novel_id}.xml` per novel — subscribe in any podcast app
- Clear error surfacing: every failed job shows the exact error message with a Retry button
- `replit.md` documents all env vars and how to run

    "Sec-Fetch-User": "?1",
    "Cache-Control": "max-age=0",
}

def extract_chapter(url: str, html: Optional[str] = None) -> ChapterText:
    """Main entry point. Fetch (if needed), extract, clean and return ChapterText.

    Captcha detection is intentionally deferred: we always attempt extraction
    first. Only if extraction yields too little text AND captcha markers are
    present do we raise CaptchaError. This avoids false positives on sites like
    fanmtl.com / wuxiaspot.com where Cloudflare injects challenge scripts into
    the HTML while the chapter content is still present on the page.
    """
    if html is None:
        html = fetch_html(url)

    title = extract_chapter_title(html)
    next_url = detect_next_chapter_url(html, url)
    novel_info = detect_novel_info(html, url)

    text = extract_with_known_selectors(html)
    if not text:
        text = extract_with_trafilatura(html)
    if not text or len(text) < 200:
        text = extract_with_bs4_heuristic(html)

    text = clean_text(text or "")

    if len(text) < 100:
        # Only now check for captcha — extraction already failed
        if is_captcha_page(html):
            raise CaptchaError(
                "Could not extract chapter text and a captcha/challenge page was detected. "
                "Open the URL in your browser to solve it, then retry."
            )
        raise ValueError(
            "Could not find substantial chapter text on this page. "
            f"Tried all extraction strategies on: {url}"
        )

    return ChapterText(
        title=title or "Untitled Chapter",
        text=text,
        word_count=len(text.split()),
        source_url=url,
        next_chapter_url=next_url,
        novel_title=novel_info.get("title"),
        novel_index_url=novel_info.get("index_url"),
    )
